CheckTitle: AI agent cross-account access must not exceed 3 accounts
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Permissions**. Checks whether an AI agent has cross-account trust relationships and, if so, whether the number of accessible accounts exceeds three. Cross-account access multiplies the blast radius of a compromised agent — each additional account the agent can reach is a potential lateral-movement target.
Risk: An agent with cross-account access to many accounts can be leveraged as a pivot point to compromise the entire AWS organization. A prompt-injection or supply-chain attack against one agent could trigger data exfiltration or resource destruction across dozens of accounts simultaneously.

Code.Other:
1. List all roles the agent can assume cross-account: review trust policies and SCPs.
2. Identify the business justification for each cross-account relationship.
3. Remove trust relationships to accounts that are not strictly required.
4. Limit remaining trust relationships to specific roles via the `Principal` condition.
5. Add an `aws:PrincipalAccount` condition to restrict which accounts can be assumed.
6. Monitor cross-account assume-role events via CloudTrail and alert on unexpected account targets.

Recommendation.Text: Restrict cross-account trust to the minimum set of accounts required (three or fewer). Use AWS Organizations SCPs to deny `sts:AssumeRole` to accounts outside an approved allowlist. Add `aws:PrincipalAccount` and `aws:PrincipalOrgID` conditions to all cross-account trust policies. Audit cross-account relationships quarterly.

Notes: ASPM-017 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
